title: Electronic word-of-mouth via consumer-opinion platforms: What motivates consumers to articulate themselves on the Internet?
what_it_supports: Concern for others, self-enhancement, social benefits
key_takeaway: Concern for other consumers, social interaction, economic incentives, and self-worth enhancement are primary eWOM drivers.

title: What Motivates Consumers to Write Online Travel Reviews?
key_takeaway: Travel review writers are mainly motivated by helping providers, concern for other consumers, and enjoyment/positive self-enhancement.

title: Online travel review posting intentions: a social exchange theory perspective
key_takeaway: Altruism, reputation, rewards, and venting increase intent, while cognitive and executional costs reduce online travel review posting intentions.

title: What motivates posting online travel reviews? Integrating gratifications with technological acceptance factors
what_it_supports: Habit, ease of use, and low-friction design
key_takeaway: Altruism, reciprocity, effort expectancy, habit, and hedonic motivation positively influence review posting; practitioners should make review sites simple and easy to use.

title: Understanding Users' Urge to Post Online Reviews: A Study Based on Existence, Relatedness, and Growth Theory
key_takeaway: Helping others, supporting websites, and recognition-related motivations influence the urge to post online reviews.

title: Motivation crowding in online product reviewing: A qualitative study of Amazon reviewers
key_takeaway: Extrinsic motivation can crowd out intrinsic reviewing motivation when status recognition or reciprocal obligation becomes dominant.

title: Altruism in eWOM: Propensity to Write Reviews on Hotel Experience
venue_or_source: Journal of Theoretical and Applied Electronic Commerce Research
key_takeaway: Among hotel customers, the strongest underlying motivation to write a review is altruistic.

title: Conspicuous Reviewing: Affiliation with High-status Organizations as a Motivation for Writing Online Reviews
what_it_supports: Reviewing is partly identity and status expression
key_takeaway: Some people write reviews to make consumption visible and status-relevant.

title: Anonymity and self-expression in online rating systems—An experimental analysis
venue_or_source: Journal of Behavioral and Experimental Economics
key_takeaway: Self-expression stimulates non-altruistic subjects to publish ratings, and anonymity blunts that motive.

title: Beyond credibility: expressive authenticity judgments of online reviews
what_it_supports: Reviews are judged as authentic expressions of personal experience
key_takeaway: Consumers judge expressive authenticity separately from credibility, and expressive authenticity has its own effect on review usefulness.

title: Moderating effects of rating on text and helpfulness in online hotel reviews: an analytical approach
key_takeaway: Review rating and review text interact with each other in shaping perceptions of hotel reviews.

title: Assessing the helpfulness of online hotel reviews: A classification-based approach
key_takeaway: Online hotel review helpfulness can be investigated through review quality, sentiment, and reviewer characteristics.

title: Exploring the antecedents for hospitality reviewers’ trustworthiness and its impact on business patronage
key_takeaway: Reviewer trustworthiness depends on both qualitative and quantitative review information, including sentiment and emotional arousal in text.

title: Rant or rave: variation over time in the language of online reviews
what_it_supports: Shorter, less comprehensive review text is a risk
key_takeaway: Reviews have become less comprehensive over time; the paper also reports that mobile-submitted reviews were shorter and showed lower language quality in Booking.com data.

title: How does mobile device usage influence review helpfulness through consumer evaluation? Evidence from TripAdvisor
what_it_supports: Lower-friction input modes may reduce helpfulness
key_takeaway: Mobile reviews received fewer helpful votes, with shorter text among the mechanisms examined.
